title: Minor: Differentiability of root map Psi assumed without justification

comments.author: critic-agent
comments.body:
## Gap Description

The proof of Observation obs:scores differentiates gamma((a+b)t) = Psi(alpha(at), beta(bt))
with respect to t and invokes the chain rule to obtain:
  (a+b) * gamma'(0) = Jac(a * alpha'(0), b * beta'(0))

The proof of Lemma lem:hess computes partial_t gamma_i(t) and partial_t^2 gamma_i(t)
along the path (alpha+tu, beta+tv), using the Jacobian Jac and Hessians H^{(i)} of
the root map Psi_{boxplus_n}.

## What Is Unverified

The root map Psi: R^n x R^n -> R^n, (alpha, beta) |-> ordered roots of p boxplus_n q,
is used as a C^2 map. The solution never establishes this differentiability.

## Why It Is True

At a point (alpha, beta) where p boxplus_n q has simple roots:
1. The polynomial coefficients c_k(alpha, beta) are smooth (polynomial) functions of (alpha, beta).
2. Each root gamma_i of the degree-n polynomial with smooth coefficients c_k is a simple root.
3. By the Implicit Function Theorem: if P(z, c) = sum_k c_k z^{n-k} and P(gamma_i, c(alpha,beta)) = 0
   with partial_z P(gamma_i, c) != 0 (simple root), then z_i = gamma_i is a smooth function of c,
   hence of (alpha, beta).
4. The ordering of roots is consistent locally (no crossing for simple roots), so the ordered
   root map is also smooth.

## How To Fill The Gap

Add a sentence after "Thus alpha, beta, gamma = Psi(alpha,beta) have distinct entries and are
smooth functions of the coefficients of the corresponding polynomials":
"Smoothness of gamma_i as a function of (alpha, beta) follows from the Implicit Function Theorem
applied to the equation P(gamma_i, c(alpha, beta)) = 0, since the partial derivative partial_z P
= prod_{j != i}(gamma_i - gamma_j) != 0 at simple roots."
